name: cpig-kin-18-2link-arm
description: kin-18 2-DOF serial arm — first serial-chain (open kinematic chain, NO closed loops) fixture in the kinematics suite. Planar 2-link arm: world + upper_arm + forearm; shoulder revolute + elbow revolute, both driven by simultaneous constant torques (80 N·mm + 60 N·mm). Forward-dynamics integrates 2s; 5 Pose at Step taps flatten into Pose Bound Bodies, rendering both arm segments at all five sampled poses simultaneously so the end-effector trajectory is visible as a fanned strip of arms. Topological complement to the closed-loop walkers (kin_11/12). Phase 4.6.F.5 — clears the Session A ≥5-fixture minimum.

setup.vlmDescription: A 2-DOF serial robotic arm shown as a stop-motion strip at five different time points of a 2-second forward-dynamics integration. The arm has TWO bound bodies: the upper_arm (rendered as a thicker pipe radius 5 mm from the world-origin shoulder to the elbow at design-pose world (100,0,0)) and the forearm (a thinner pipe radius 4 mm from the elbow to the end-effector at (180,0,0)). Under simultaneous constant torques (80 N·mm at the shoulder, 60 N·mm at the elbow), both joints rotate; the end-effector sweeps a curved 2D path (a logarithmic-spiral-like locus) through the arm's workspace. All five sampled poses (steps 0/500/1000/1500/2000) are rendered together, so the strip shows the arm fanning out from its initial straight-out +X configuration into progressively more bent poses. A faint design-pose skeleton (shoulder and elbow joint glyphs + arm skeleton lines) is overlaid via Preview Diagram in Display Mode 2.
